title: Actuator, Micrometer & Observability
summary: Spring Boot Actuator endpoints, Micrometer metrics, distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry, and health indicators.

sections.content:
## Spring Boot Actuator

Actuator exposes operational endpoints for monitoring and managing your application:

```yaml
# application.yml
management:
  endpoints:
    web:
      exposure:
        include: health,info,metrics,prometheus,env,loggers
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: always
```

### Key endpoints:
| Endpoint | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `/actuator/health` | Health status (UP/DOWN) with component details |
| `/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| `/actuator/prometheus` | Prometheus-format metrics scrape |
| `/actuator/info` | Application version, git commit info |
| `/actuator/loggers` | View/change log levels at runtime |
| `/actuator/env` | Current environment properties |
| `/actuator/conditions` | Auto-configuration report |

sections.title: Observability Stack
sections.content:
graph LR
    App[Spring Boot App] -->|metrics| Prom[Prometheus]
    App -->|traces| OTEL[OpenTelemetry Collector]
    App -->|logs| Loki[Loki / ELK]
    Prom -->|visualize| Grafana[Grafana]
    OTEL -->|traces| Jaeger[Jaeger / Tempo]
    Loki -->|visualize| Grafana
    Jaeger -->|visualize| Grafana
    style App fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Grafana fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Distributed Tracing with OpenTelemetry

Spring Boot 3.x has first-class OpenTelemetry support via Micrometer Tracing:

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>io.micrometer</groupId>
    <artifactId>micrometer-tracing-bridge-otel</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
    <groupId>io.opentelemetry.instrumentation</groupId>
    <artifactId>opentelemetry-spring-boot-starter</artifactId>
</dependency>
```

```yaml
management:
  tracing:
    sampling:
      probability: 1.0  # sample 100% in dev, ~1% in prod
  otlp:
    tracing:
      endpoint: http://otel-collector:4318/v1/traces
```

All HTTP requests are automatically traced with trace IDs propagated via headers (`traceparent`).

sections.title: Three Pillars of Observability
sections.content:
graph TD
    O[Observability]
    O --> M[Metrics\nWhat is happening?\nCounters, Gauges, Timers\nMicrometer → Prometheus]
    O --> L[Logs\nWhat happened?\nStructured events\nLogback → Loki/ELK]
    O --> T[Traces\nWhere did it happen?\nRequest journey\nOTel → Jaeger/Tempo]
    M --> Grafana[Grafana Dashboard]
    L --> Grafana
    T --> Grafana
    style O fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Grafana fill:#059669,color:#fff

sections.question: What is Micrometer's role in Spring Boot observability?
options: It is a logging framework that replaces SLF4J
It is a metrics facade that abstracts over different monitoring backends like Prometheus, Datadog, CloudWatch
It is a tracing agent that instruments bytecode automatically
It is Spring's replacement for Java Flight Recorder
sections.explanation: Micrometer is a vendor-neutral metrics facade — like SLF4J but for metrics. You write metrics code once using Micrometer's API, and configure which backend to export to (Prometheus, Datadog, InfluxDB, CloudWatch, etc.) via dependencies and config, without changing application code.
